verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Surfline and Surf-Forecast are poor/zero, and wind is above your normal limit.

conversationalSummary.text: Tomorrow is probably one to skip. Baleal at After work (17:30 start) looks like the least-bad option, but not really something worth chasing. The wind looks like the main issue and could easily make it messy. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam first, but unless it looks surprisingly clean, I'd save the drive.

best.windFit: N 30 km/h · onshore · too windy, strong
best.bestWind: ideal E/ESE/SE, usable NE/ENE/SSE, clean <=25 km/h

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Baleal looks like a fun-sized window for Safe fun, Low confidence: 0.9-1.2 m, 227 kJ, 9 s period, consistent 71/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push.  Watch: wind is over 30 km/h; wind fit is poor or too strong; 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the Baleal sandbanks; gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy.
best.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for after work (17:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is part of the picture, but not the deciding factor.
warnings: wind fit is poor or too strong
6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the Baleal sandbanks
gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy
